episodes.title: Olive Leaf- Breaking Free Event
episodes.description:
The Movement Has Begun: Breaking Free at The Food Connect Shed 

This wasn't just an event; it was a shift in the atmosphere. The Olive Leaf Network x Stories that Stir event in Salisbury was unlike anything I’ve ever experienced. To sit in a room filled with such strong, courageous, and motivated people—all ready to influence real change—was a profound privilege.

The energy was electric. Seeing survivors from so many different backgrounds connecting, finding long-lost faces, and realizing they aren't alone... this is a movement in motion.

A Heartfelt Thank You
A massive thank you to  Our Incredible Storytellers
Thank you for your raw, gut-wrenching, and beautiful truth-telling. Your voices are the catalysts for the accountability we’ve been waiting for.

Dani Sorensen
Matt Christopher
Jayde Struhs
Aaron Sharp
Helen Pomery
Gracie Thrush

Why This Matters Now
This is our "#MeToo" moment. The time for truth-telling, accountability, and systemic change is now. We are already seeing the ripple effect, with an influx of feedback and interview requests pouring in. People are finally listening.

A Note on Our Mission: Our intent is never to oppose religion, but rather to support it. Much like speaking out against domestic violence honors the sanctity of marriage, we believe that addressing these issues head-on is the best way to foster truly safe and flourishing faith communities.

The Journey Continues
The journey of "Breaking Free" doesn't end when the lights go down; it’s just the beginning. We need to keep this momentum going.

episodes.title: Public Hearing for Inquiry into Cults
episodes.description:
Directors of the Olive Leaf Network, Mirriam Francis and Maria Esguerra attend public hearing at Parliament House for government inquiry into cults, 5th November 2025.

In 2025, the Parliament of Victoria in Australia announced an inquiry into cults and organised fringe groups, to examine the methods used to recruit and control members and the impacts of coercive control. The Committee will report its findings and recommendations by 30 September 2026.
